mainEntity.name: Can I sue my company for my back injury or am I stuck with workers' comp?

acceptedAnswer.text: Generally, you cannot sue your employer because of the 'exclusive remedy' rule. However, you may be able to sue a third party (like a contractor or equipment manufacturer) if their negligence caused your injury.
acceptedAnswer.description:
In Utah and Idaho, you usually cannot sue your employer for a work injury due to the 'exclusive remedy' rule. This rule protects employers from lawsuits in exchange for providing no-fault insurance. However, you can sue a 'third party' if someone else caused your back injury. For example, if you were driving for work in Ogden and got rear-ended, or if a defective forklift caused your injury, you could have both a work comp claim and a personal injury lawsuit.

Key factors include who was at fault and whether they are your employer or an outside entity.

Identify everyone involved in the accident. If a contractor or a manufacturer is at fault, your total compensation could be much higher than work comp alone.

Distinguishing between a standard work comp claim and a third-party lawsuit is critical for maximizing your financial recovery.
